creator: saper@saper.info
text:
Now I think this is not a regression... ACPI version behaves better but sometimes the keyboard driver is not attached due to "SPI Error: 5"

Typing on the keyboard is maddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddde difficult with "keeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeyy storms" accompanied by

Jan 30 15:00:32 radziecki kernel: spi0: transfer timeout
Jan 30 15:00:32 radziecki kernel: atopcase0: SPI error: 5
Jan 30 15:00:39 radziecki kernel: spi0: transfer timeout
Jan 30 15:00:39 radziecki kernel: atopcase0: SPI error: 5
Jan 30 15:00:45 radziecki kernel: spi0: transfer timeout
Jan 30 15:00:45 radziecki kernel: atopcase0: SPI error: 5
Jan 30 15:00:52 radziecki kernel: spi0: transfer timeout
Jan 30 15:00:52 radziecki kernel: atopcase0: SPI error: 5
Jan 30 15:00:58 radziecki kernel: spi0: transfer timeout
Jan 30 15:00:58 radziecki kernel: atopcase0: SPI error: 5


My SPI controller is this:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

spi0@pci0:0:30:3:	class=0x118000 rev=0x21 hdr=0x00 vendor=0x8086 device=0x9d2a subvendor=0x8086 subdevice=0x7270

Maybe it is a generic SPI problem                                    here (on Intel Skylake)
